summary: The CI job failed because a git push to GitHub could not be completed due to a network or transport error (exit code 128). The fatal error 'unable to access https://github.com/vercel/next.js/' with 'Unknown system error -128' indicates a transient network failure or a credential/auth issue during the git operation.
root_cause: A git command failed with exit code 128 and 'Unknown system error -128' when attempting to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/', indicating a transient network or transport failure prevented the git push/access operation from completing.

evidence.reason: This is the direct git error indicating the remote repository could not be reached over HTTPS.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128)...
evidence.reason: Exit code 128 from git typically signals a fatal git error, often network-related; 'Unknown system error -128' suggests a low-level transport failure.
This non-standard system error code further supports a transient or environmental network issue rather than a configuration or authentication problem.
Confirms the overall job step exited with a failure status after the git transport error.

suggested_fix: Re-run the failed job to determine if this is a transient network issue. If failures persist, check GitHub Actions runner network connectivity and verify that any tokens or credentials used for HTTPS git access are valid and not expired.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
